content.text:
Checking whether the filter popover needs JS updates for fixed mobile positioning:

[REDACTED]

Adding a few more mobile fixes for overflow and header popovers.

[REDACTED]

Made the panel mobile-responsive with CSS-only changes — no JS or backend edits.

### What changed

**Viewport & safe areas**
- Added `viewport-fit=cover` on both `index.html` and `admin.html`
- Layout padding respects notch/home-indicator insets via `env(safe-area-inset-*)`

**Header (≤720px / ≤520px)**
- Country/ATS/location selects reflow into a 2-column grid, then stack on small phones
- Action buttons wrap; icon-only mode below 520px (existing behavior kept)
- Popovers and user menu stay within the screen

**Filter bar**
- Search goes full width; sort + filters stack on narrow screens
- Filter popover becomes a bottom sheet (fixed, thumb-friendly)

**Job cards**
- Company header stacks (badges/actions below name instead of a cramped side column)
- Position cards stack title + ATS score vertically
- Action buttons get larger tap targets on touch devices

**Dialogs & fetch panel**
- Bottom-sheet style on mobile (full width, rounded top corners)
- Dialog footer buttons stack full-width on small screens

**Admin panel**
- Header stacks; fetch controls go single-column; tables stay horizontally scrollable

**Misc**
- Toast centered above the safe area
- `overflow-x: clip` on `body` to avoid horizontal scroll
- Long careers URLs wrap instead of overflowing

Cache bust: CSS bumped to `v=25` in both HTML files.

Hard-refresh the panel (or open devtools → mobile viewport) to verify. If you want a specific breakpoint tuned further — e.g. header selects always in one row on tablet — say which device width you care about.

[REDACTED]

<user_query>

content.text:
You were right to push back — the first pass was a pile of breakpoint patches, not a mobile design. Here’s what changed with a full pass across every surface.

### Approach

Mobile needs three layers working together, not just CSS tweaks:

1. **Structure** — header split into title/account row + catalog controls row (no more absolute-position avatar hack)
2. **Interaction** — bottom sheets with backdrop + scroll lock for filters, ATS score, and hide-reason popovers
3. **Layout system** — consistent grids for stats, company toolbars, position actions, admin tables

### What’s covered now

| Area | Mobile behavior |
|------|-----------------|
| **Header** | Title + avatar on one row; country/ATS/location in a grid; actions in a full-width button grid |
| **Stats** | 2-column grid (applied-today spans full width) instead of horizontal scroll |
| **Filter bar** | Stacked search + sort/filters; filter panel opens as bottom sheet with backdrop |
| **Company cards** | Header stacks; toolbar in 2-col grid; careers URLs wrap |
| **Position cards** | Title/ATS stack; action buttons in 2-col grid (full-width on small phones) |
| **Dialogs & fetch panel** | Bottom sheets, full width, safe-area aware |
| **Popovers** | ATS score + hide-reason use bottom sheets on ≤720px (JS + CSS) |
| **Admin** | Stats grid, stacked header actions, **card-style tables** with `data-label` (no horizontal scroll trap) |
| **Login** | Full-width actions, reduced margins |
| **Touch** | 2.5rem min heights on controls; larger chips/toggles |

### Files touched

- `index.html` — header restructure, `filterBackdrop`
- `styles.css` — header base layout, `stat-card` (was missing for admin), unified mobile system
- `filters.js`, `events.js`, `utils.js` — sheet behavior + `isNarrowViewport()` + scroll lock
- `admin.js` — responsive table cells with labels

Hard-refresh both `/` and `/admin` (CSS `v=26`, JS `v=74`/`v=5`) and test at 375px and 768px. If a specific screen still feels wrong, tell me which view and device width.

[REDACTED]

<user_query>
it was drastically bad, because of the UX you used.
its fine now i will change this to something correct.
</user_query>

Fair call. Cramming desktop patterns into mobile grids and bottom sheets was the wrong UX bet — it “worked” at breakpoints but felt wrong to use. Glad you’ve got it to a better place now.

If you want a second pair of eyes on your approach before you ship it, I’m happy to review.

[REDACTED]
